_patch_meta.description: Dimension Review Phase C — C04 cluster. 136 dimension assignments (CLAUDE_AI confidence). 12 automated label corrections. 6 Session B findings. 3 Session D pointers. 31 groups deferred (QA-VAGUE, QA-BROAD, QA-REVIEW, QA-EXTERNALISED) — require follow-up session.

record.finding: Registry 8 (appetite) contributes zero desiderative groups — all 12 groups belong to the life/vitality semantic domain (Hebrew chai/chayah family). The appetite register, as extracted, has no desiderative content. Session B should establish what the appetite registry actually contributes to C04 inner-being analysis.

Registry 43 (desire) shows a consistent divine/human register split: divine desire, will, and longing = Theological/Divine-Human; human desire and will = Volitional/Will or desiderative. These two registers appear throughout the vocabulary. Session B should read them in parallel and assess how Scripture's account of divine desire relates to human desire.

Misdirected desire dominates a large segment of Registry 43 — 10+ groups (464-001, 469-001, 480-001, 481-001, 481-003, 492-003, 497-001, 498-001, 504-001, 505-001) name desire as moral disorder. Session B should examine whether Scripture treats desire's moral valence as its primary theological concern, and how the disordered and rightly-ordered forms relate.

Group 513-001 names the divided inner will — the person wills good but does what they do not will (Romans 7 pattern). This is analytically foundational for understanding desire's relationship to action. Session B should give this sustained attention as a key inner-being mechanism.

Hope in Registry 78 divides into three distinct modes: (a) hope as orientation or act directed toward God (Spiritual/God-ward — 30 groups across cluster); (b) hope as a constitutive inner quality or capacity of the person (Character/Disposition — groups 385-001, 401-002, 402-001, 968-001); (c) hope's absence, collapse, or disappointment (Affective/Emotional). Session B should assess whether these are three aspects of one dimension or three distinct inner-being engagements.

Registry 115 (passion) contributes zero desiderative groups in Phase C. Group 1023-001 names defiling passion as moral drive; 5948-001 names moral defilement; 1432-001 (deferred) names patient attentiveness. Session B should clarify what the passion registry actually covers in its term set and whether the English label accurately reflects the Hebrew/Greek semantic range.

record.description: Programme-wide: groups where God is the subject of a volitional or desiderative act are systematically misclassified as Volitional/Will or Spiritual/God-ward. In C04, 8 groups were corrected (456-003, 479-001, 479-002, 502-002, 513-002, 514-002 and others). This correction pattern likely applies across all clusters. Session D should audit all clusters for this systematic label error.

C04 desiderative density is 62% — 65 of 172 groups carry non-desiderative primary content. Question: is the desiderative vocabulary of Scripture proportionally smaller than the volitional-relational vocabulary, or are there programme coverage gaps? Session D should cross-check C04 terms against other clusters and assess whether key desiderative terms are absent from the programme or misassigned.

The trust/refuge vocabulary of Registry 78 (hope) and the desiderative vocabulary of Registry 43 (desire) share the inner-being ground of orientation toward an object. Session D should examine the relationship between hope/trust and desire/longing as paired inner-being orientations, and how fear/reverence (other clusters) functions as their counterpart.
